name: Brenden Bishop
title: Builder & Technologist
intro: Husband, father, and technologist. Software Engineering leader and chronic entrepreneur. I love building, tinkering, and learning new things. Addicted to coffee and coding.
bio:
I build products and care a lot about how they get built.

Over the past decade, I've worked across engineering, product, and systems — building client-facing platforms, scaling teams, and launching my own products from 0→1. I'm most interested in the tools and workflows that actually determine how software gets designed, built, and shipped.

More recently, I've been focused on how AI changes that process. I've been integrating LLMs into real development workflows, building internal tools and agentic systems, and defining how they fit into the SDLC in practice.

This has changed how I think about system design, iteration speed, and what good development workflows look like.

Outside of my role at Morgan Stanley, I've founded and built products like Forwheel and Rbitrage Capital. I still spend a lot of my time building, experimenting, and trying to get faster at turning ideas into working software.
